name: Erl

aliases.name: Url
aliases.type: { protocol : String, host : String, port_ : Maybe Int, pathname : String, query : Query, hash : String }

values.name: new
values.comment:
Generate an empty Url record

    Erl.new ==

    { protocol = ""
    , host = ""
    , port_ = Nothing
    , pathname = ""
    , query = []
    , hash = ""
    }

values.name: parse
values.comment:
Parse an url into a Url record

    Erl.parse "http://hello.com/users/1?k=1&q=2#a/b"

    Ok
        { protocol = "http",
        , host = "hello.com",
        , port_ = Just 2000,
        , pathname = "/users/1",
        , query = [ ("k", "1"), ("q", "2") ]
        , hash = "a/b",
        }

values.name: toAbsoluteString
values.comment:
Generate a url that starts at the path

    url = { protocol = "http",
          , host = "www.hello.com",
          , port_ = Just 2000,
          , pathname = "/users/1",
          , query = [ ("k", "1"), ("q", "2") ]
          , hash = "a/b",
          }

    Erl.toAbsoluteString url == "/users/1?k=1&q=2#a/b"

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Generate a url string from an Url record

    url = { protocol = "http",
          , host = "www.hello.com",
          , port_ = Just 2000,
          , pathname = "/users/1",
          , query = [ ("k", "1"), ("q", "2") ]
          , hash = "a/b",
          }

    Erl.toString url == "http://www.hello.com:2000/users/1?k=1&q=2#a/b"

name: Erl.Query

values.name: add
values.comment:
Adds key/value in query string

    Erl.Query.add key value query

This doesn't replace existing keys, so if this is a duplicated this key is just added.

values.name: getValuesForKey
values.comment:
Gets values for a key in the query

    url = Erl.parse "?a=1&b=2&a=3"

    Erl.Query.getQueryValuesForKey "a" url.query

    == ["1", "3"]

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Removes key from query string

    Erl.Query.remove key query

values.name: set
values.comment:
Set key/value in query string, removes any existing one if necessary.

    Erl.Query.set key value query

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Convert to a string, this includes '?'

    Erl.Query.toString query == "?a=1&b=2"
